revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[九十九里平野]] to [[Kujukuri Plain]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Yokoshiba Chiba.jpg|thumb|250px|横芝駅付近を上空から東北東方向に写した写真。中央を左から右に栗山川が流れる。写真上側では、住宅地や水田が海岸線に平行（写真では右下-左上方向）に並ぶ様子が見て取れる。]] {{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | 九十九里平野と房総半島周辺の地形}} '''九十九里平野'''（くじゅうくりへいや）は、[[房総半島]]北東部、[[九十九里浜]]の背後に広がる[[平野]]である。[[旭市]]、[[匝瑳市]]、[[山武郡]][[横芝光町]]、[[山武市]]、[[東金市]]、[[九十九里町]]、[[大網白里市]]、[[茂原市]]、[[長生郡]][[白子町]]、[[長生村]]、[[長南町]]、[[睦沢町]]、[[一宮町]]の6市6町1村に跨り、面積は約900[[平方キロメートル]] で[[千葉県]]の20[[パーセント]]を占める<ref name="kd" />。
== 地理 == [[ファイル:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|1926年時点の関東平野地図に、縄文海進時代の海進領域（斜線部）を重ねた地図]] 九十九里平野は南北に約60キロメートル、東西に約10キロメートルにわたる[[海岸平野]]で、九十九里沖積低地とも呼ばれ<ref group="注">ここでの『沖積』は、[[時代区分]]における沖積世（現在は[[完新世]]と呼ばれる）を意味している。</ref>、九十九里浜の[[海岸]]線に沿って南西から北東に細長く延びる、弧状に曲がった細長い長方形をしている。北東端は[[旭市]]の[[刑部岬]]付近から、南端は[[いすみ市]]の[[太東崎]]付近で、北側は[[下総台地]]、西側は[[房総丘陵]]に接する。標高は、最高点でも10メートル以下で、海岸線と平行していくつかの旧[[砂丘]]列が見られる。
現在平野となっている部分は、[[縄文海進]]の後に海岸が後退し作られたものであり、[[中世#日本|中世]]以前には[[玉の浦]]と呼ばれていた[[海]]であった。そのため、現在でも平野に散在する池沼にわずかに含まれる塩分濃度が、内陸から海岸にかけて勾配を示すことが知られる。
現在は海岸の後退は止まり、むしろ砂浜の減少すらみられる。この原因は堆積土砂の供給源である[[利根川]]の河川改修の結果、流下する土砂が減少したことと、海食崖の[[屏風ヶ浦]]や大東崎での人間による侵食防止策の影響である。
== 気候 == 日本の[[太平洋]]側に位置する地域の代表例であり、[[気温]]は南部の海沿いであるため温暖で日較差・年較差が小さい。[[ケッペンの気候区分]]とは独立した日本独自の気候区分では[[太平洋側気候|太平洋岸式気候]]である。ヒートアイランドの影響もうけておらず、冬暖かく、夏涼しい、過ごしやすい気候となっている。そのため[[避暑地]]（海開き）及び避寒地として訪れる観光客も多い。
== 資源 == 可採埋蔵量が3,750億[[立方メートル|m<sup>3</sup>]] にも達する、日本最大の水溶性天然[[ガス田]]である[[南関東ガス田]]の中にあり、[[天然ガス]]を産出する。また、水溶性天然ガスは岩石層中の[[鹹水]]と呼ばれる地層水にガスが溶けた状態で存在しているものであるが、南関東ガス田の鹹水には[[海水]]の約2,000倍の[[ヨウ素]]が含まれ、天然ガスと同時にヨウ素も産出されている<ref group="注">南関東ガス田の鹹水から産出されるヨウ素は、一時は世界のヨウ素産出量の4割近くを占め長らく日本は世界最大のヨウ素産出国であった。現在は[[チリ硝石]]からヨウ素を産出する[[チリ]]に次いで2位となっている。</ref>。
なお、南関東ガス田は[[南関東]]に広く分布しているものであり、[[1970年代]]（[[昭和]]45年頃）までは[[東京湾]]岸でも天然ガスを採掘していた。しかし[[地盤沈下]]を招き、その影響が深刻なため東京湾岸では採掘業者から鉱区権を買い取り採掘を停止している。九十九里平野でも地盤沈下の影響は避けられないが、自治体と採掘業者による協定のもとで採掘事業を行っている。市町村営で[[都市ガス]]の供給を行っている自治体も多い。
ガス層までの深度が低いところでは、しばしば地面の割れ目から自然に噴出し、何らかの要因で引火することがある。[[2004年]]（[[平成]]16年）に起こった[[九十九里いわし博物館]]の爆発事故は自然湧出したガスに引火したことが原因と見られている。
天然ガスに比すべきではないが、鉱物資源としていま一つ[[砂鉄]]がある。九十九里平野は海底が隆起したことに加え、[[犬吠埼]]方面からの[[砂洲]]の形成によって陸地となったものであり、[[比重]]の小さい砂は[[潮流]]に運ばれ南部まで達するが、含まれている砂鉄は比重が大きいので北に残る。[[飯岡町|飯岡]]付近の砂には高濃度で砂鉄が含まれており、過去においてはこれを採掘していた（現在は採掘していない）。[[1964年]]（昭和39年）には198,000トンの採掘が行われ、[[東邦化学工業]]千葉工場に供給された<ref> 『日本地誌 第8巻 千葉県・神奈川県』 53頁</ref>。
== 土地の活用 == === 水系と漁港 === * 河川：[[栗山川]]（[[流域面積]]：292km{{sup|2}}）・[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]（72km{{sup|2}}）・[[作田川]]（104km{{sup|2}}）・[[真亀川]]（82km{{sup|2}}）・[[南白亀川]]（116km{{sup|2}}）・[[一宮川]]（222km{{sup|2}}） * 湿地：栗山川湿地（[[多古光湿原]]）・[[成東・東金食虫植物群落]]・[[茂原・八積湿原]]（消滅） * 海跡湖：[[椿海]]（消滅）・[[乾草沼]]・[[坂田池]]・[[鳥喰沼]]（消滅） * ため池：[[雄蛇ヶ池]]・[[洞庭湖 (千葉県)|洞庭湖]]・[[小中池]] * 用水路：[[大利根用水]]・[[両総用水]]・[[房総導水路]] * 漁港：[[飯岡漁港]]・[[栗山川漁港]]・[[片貝漁港]]
九十九里平野の中央に位置する[[栗山川]]中流域には[[縄文時代]]の[[ラグーン]]が湖沼群として残る地域があり栗山川[[湿地]]と呼ばれる。ここは絶滅が危惧される貴重な[[湿地植物]]の生育地域であるが、[[明治]]以降の開発により急速にその面積を減らしている<ref group="注">かつて九十九里平野にあった多くの[[海跡湖]]は姿を消したが、南房総から続く房総台地が山武市で終わり九十九里平野が下総台地の方に拡がっている栗山川中流域の、[[多古町]]南部・匝瑳市北西部・横芝光町北部にかけての一帯には、[[乾草沼]]など数少ない海跡湖が残されている。</ref>。[[山武市]]と[[東金市]]の境界の[[作田川]]中流域にある[[成東・東金食虫植物群落]]は、絶滅が危惧されている[[食虫植物]]などの湿地植物の群落地帯として、[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。九十九里平野の南端に近い[[茂原市]]から[[長生村]]にかけての地域に、[[植物学者]]の[[牧野富太郎]]博士が「まさに植物の宝庫である」と絶賛した[[茂原・八積湿原]]がかつて存在していた<ref> [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/shizen/01_4.html 農林水産省 関東農政局 両総農業水利事業所 貴重な植物群生地]</ref>。
南部では、[[江戸時代]]初期には[[雄蛇ヶ池]]、その後[[洞庭湖 (千葉県)|洞庭湖]]、さらに昭和初期には[[小中池]]が設けられるなど、用水不足を補うための諸策が採られたが抜本的な策とはなりえなかった。その後、大利根用水、両総用水、房総導水路のような用水事業が行われた。また、九十九里平野の北端に接する形で[[飯岡漁港]]があり、栗山川河口には[[栗山川漁港]]が、作田川河口には[[片貝漁港]]がある。
=== 納屋集落 === 九十九里平野の特徴的な[[集落]]の形態として納屋集落が上げられる。海岸線に平行に、集落、耕作地、集落、耕作地、集落のように、集落と耕作地（[[水田]]）が帯状に列を成すものである。海岸近くで[[農業]]のかたわら[[漁業]]を営んでいた集落があり<ref group="注">関西漁民進出以前この地方では、自給的性格の強い小規模な漁業が行われているにすぎなかった。</ref>、海岸線が後退していくなどの理由から浜辺に[[漁具]]等を保管する[[納屋]]を建て通っていたものが、やがて定住するようになり浜辺に納屋集落が形成され、集落と集落の間の低地を耕作地として利用する、ということを繰り返し成立した集落の形態であり、元の集落と浜辺の集落は主従関係にあるのが普通である。このため、[[平成の大合併]]以前は、[[横芝町]]、[[成東町]]、[[大網白里市|大網白里町]]のように、細長い町域を持つ自治体が多かった<ref name="kd" />。
水に恵まれ農業が盛んであった北東側などでは、[[古墳時代]]以前の納屋集落である古村、[[奈良時代|奈良]][[平安時代|平安]]期に開発された岡集落、[[鎌倉時代]]以降の浜集落の順に形成されているが<ref group="注">北東部には、椿海の干拓で成立した村々もあった。</ref>、[[関西]][[漁民]]の[[入植]]があった南部の地域は若干様相を異にする。この地域では、江戸時代[[地引き網]]漁が盛んであったころ、[[イワシ]]の豊漁期に多くの漁民が移住し浜辺の納屋集落の[[人口]]が増加する<ref group="注">近世初頭から[[延宝]]年間（1673年-1681年）ごろまでは、旅網と呼ばれる関西漁民の季節的出稼ぎ漁が主だったが、干鰯商人の関東進出もあり[[享保]]年間（1716年-1736年）ころには旅網は跡を断った。</ref>。その後不漁期になると人口が過剰になるので、これらの漁民が背後の未墾地を開発して新田集落を形成し農民化した。水の恵みに乏しかった土地で新田開発をするので水不足が進むという問題はあるが、1年間に2メートル強の割合で海岸線が後退していくことによって成り立った集落の形態であることには変わりはない<ref> 『日本地誌 第8巻 千葉県・神奈川県』 241頁</ref>。
現在の海沿いの集落は、北東側では背後の親集落の名に「浜」を付け○○浜と呼ぶのが一般的であるが、南部は○○納屋と呼ばれる。[[干鰯]]の生産が盛んだったころ、北東側からは[[江戸]]の[[干鰯問屋|干鰯商人]]に向け陸送にて出荷した。一方、南部の○○納屋の地域は関西（大阪）の干鰯商人の勢力圏であり、おもに[[海路]][[浦賀]]へ集荷したという<ref> 『民俗地名語彙事典』 451頁</ref>。
=== 産業 === 高度は、最内陸部でも9〜10メートル、海岸砂浜で3〜6メートルという低さであり、平均斜度は極めて緩やかである<ref name="nh" />。しかし微妙に高低差があり、海岸線に平行する数条の砂堆と砂堆間の低湿地とが交互に存在し、低湿地には海跡湖が湖沼群として残されていた。このため、低湿地は容易に水田とすることが可能であり、[[奈良時代]][[藤原黒麻呂]]の開発した[[藻原荘]]は現在の茂原市の始まりといわれ、[[平安時代]]には[[平高望]]が私営田の開発を進めるなど、古くから新田開発が行われていた<ref> [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/rekishi/01.html 農林水産省 関東農政局 両総農業水利事業所 総の国の誕生]</ref>。栗山川／椿海水系の水に恵まれた中央部から北東側と、房総台地との境界が[[分水界]]となり大きな川の無い南部の地域とでは程度の差があり<ref group="注">南部でも、一宮川流域では水不足はさほど深刻ではなかった。</ref>、また[[紀伊国|紀州]][[加太町|加太浦]]の漁民によって開かれた[[九十九里町]]<ref>『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 247頁</ref>のような例外もあり、時代によっても異なるが、主たる産業は農業であり平坦なことから水田が多く[[稲作]]が主体であった<ref name="nh" />。
[[鎌倉時代]]以降も砂堆地間の低湿地の湖沼群を狭める形で新田が開発されてきた。だが、この湖沼群が下流の村にとっての水源でもあったことから、開発が進めば進むほど下流の村は水が足りなくなってしまうという事態となっていた。そして、[[近世#日本|近世]]に入り[[徳川家康]]が江戸に入府すると、膨張する江戸の町の食糧事情もあって新田開発はさらに盛んになり、椿海の干拓に見られるような大規模な新田開発も行われた<ref> [https://www.maff.go.jp/kanto/nouson/sekkei/kokuei/ryoso/rekishi/03.html 農林水産省 関東農政局 両総農業水利事業所 水をめぐる混乱]</ref>。また、江戸時代には水に恵まれず未墾地が残されていた南部を中心に紀州漁民の入植も盛んになり、九十九里浜といえば地引き網によるイワシ漁のようにいわれ活況を呈した<ref name="kd" />。
明治維新後機械揚繰網が登場すると、遠浅な砂浜海岸であり良港に恵まれず[[漁船]]の大型化への対応がむずかしかった九十九里浜の漁業の衰退は著しく、漁業から農業への転換が進められた<ref name="nh" />。特に、水に恵まれず農業に適さなかったことから漁業によって発展した南部の地域が、農業への転換を余儀なくされたことは深刻であった。江戸時代のはじめに椿海の干拓が行われた北東部や、[[大正時代]]から[[山武郡東部連合耕地整理組合]]の用水事業が行われていた中央部とでは、それぞれ事情は異なるものの、南部の地域に限らず九十九里平野の農業用水不足は深刻化していった。
また、北側の利根川岸一帯では[[利根川東遷事業|東遷事業]]や[[中条堤]]破堤の影響から[[水害]]が頻発していた。加えて、戦時の食料不足の折、食料増産の必要性も大きく、[[大利根用水]]と[[両総用水]]の二つの用水事業が計画され、[[1935年]]（昭和10年）および[[1943年]]（昭和18年）に着工した。戦争の激化に伴う一事中断を経て、二用水は終戦後の[[1950年]]（昭和25年）と[[1965年]]（昭和40年）にそれぞれ竣工し、ようやく九十九里平野に安定した農業用水が供給されるようになった<ref name="nh" />。
しかし、その頃はすでに[[日本人]]の[[食事]]の欧風化が進行しており米離れに拍車がかかっていた。このため[[1970年]]（昭和45年）以降は[[減反政策]]といわれる生産調整政策がとられ[[転作]]が奨励された。一貫して[[米]]の増産を進めてきた九十九里平野の水田稲作に特化した農業は、他の作物への転作は容易ではなかったが、[[都心]]に近いということもあって生鮮野菜の栽培などの他、[[観光産業]]への転換も図られた<ref name="nh">『日本地誌 第8巻 千葉県・神奈川県』 228頁</ref>。[[交通]]の便の良いところは[[都市化]]が進んでいる。その他、天然ガスが豊富なことから[[茂原市]]周辺では鉱工業も盛んである<ref>『日本地誌 第8巻 千葉県・神奈川県』 243頁</ref>。
[[真空管]]の製造に必要なメタンが入手できるため、1936年には理研真空工業が真空管と電球の工場を建設した。[[日立製作所|日立]]と合併し日立製作所茂原工場となり、1958年から[[ブラウン管]]ディスプレイの製造を開始した。茂原市には液晶の触媒に利用するヨードのメーカーが集積し入手性が良いため、1974年から電卓用の液晶パネル、1994年から液晶ディスプレイの製造を開始した。工場は[[日立ディスプレイズ]]を経て[[ジャパンディスプレイ]]の工場となっている。
== 交通 == [[日本]]では一般に[[山]]や[[谷]]など[[交通]]の障害になる[[地形]]が多く、その地形に配慮したルートが採られるが、平坦地の続く九十九里平野ではそのような障害はなく、ルートは海岸線に[[平行]]または[[垂直]]になる。
=== 平行な交通 === [[主要地方道]]の[[千葉県道30号飯岡一宮線]]を除き複数の路線となるが、合計は概ね60キロメートルである。 ;鉄道 [[総武本線]]（飯岡-成東）、[[東金線]]（成東-大網）、[[外房線]]（大網-上総一ノ宮） :[[台地]]麓の古村を結び、[[飯岡駅|飯岡]]、[[旭駅 (千葉県)|旭]]、[[八日市場駅|八日市場]]、[[横芝駅|横芝]]、[[松尾駅 (千葉県)|松尾]]、[[成東駅|成東]]、[[東金駅|東金]]、[[大網駅|大網]]、[[本納駅|本納]]、[[茂原駅|茂原]]、[[上総一ノ宮駅|一宮]]の商業中心地が4〜5キロメートルで並び、[[商圏]]は平行方向に発達している<ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。 ;国道 [[鉄道路線]]と重なり合って、[[国道126号]]、[[国道128号|128号]]が北から南までを結んでいる。なお平野内ではなく背後の台地に位置するが、[[首都圏中央連絡自動車道|圏央道]]の[[茂原長南インターチェンジ]]から[[松尾横芝インターチェンジ]]と、[[銚子連絡道路]]の[[横芝光インターチェンジ]]までの区間も、国道128号、126号の[[バイパス道路|バイパス]]として、九十九里平野の交通に寄与している。 ;主要地方道・他 [[千葉県道30号飯岡一宮線]]が浜集落を北から南に結んでいる。このうち片貝から一宮までは[[有料道路]]の[[九十九里有料道路|波乗り道路]]が並行している。また、[[千葉県道408号飯岡九十九里自転車道線]]と[[千葉県道405号九十九里一宮大原自転車道線]]も並行している。 ;一般県道 [[千葉県道122号飯岡片貝線]]と[[千葉県道123号一宮片貝線]]が、飯岡から一宮までの間の岡集落を結んでいる。
=== 垂直な交通 === 鉄道や国道沿いの商業中心地と、海岸の千葉県道30号飯岡一宮線の間の路線で、距離は多くとも10キロメートル程度であるが、主従関係にある古村、岡集落、浜集落を結ぶルートとして発達している。 :主要地方道は北から、[[千葉県道48号八日市場野栄線]]、[[千葉県道49号八日市場栄線]]、[[千葉県道78号横芝上堺線]]、[[千葉県道58号松尾蓮沼線]]、[[千葉県道25号東金片貝線]]、[[千葉県道75号東金豊海線]]、[[千葉県道83号山田台大網白里線]]、[[千葉県道31号茂原白子線]]、[[千葉県道84号茂原長生線]]があり、千葉県道75号東金豊海線のバイパスとして[[東金九十九里有料道路]]がある。
=== バス路線 === 横芝光町から北東側は[[千葉交通]]、山武市付近は[[ちばフラワーバス]]、東金市付近は[[九十九里鉄道]]、大網白里市以南は[[小湊鉄道]]の[[路線バス]]がある。海岸に垂直方向の結び付きが強いため、平行に北と南を結ぶような路線は無い。
[[旭市コミュニティバス]]、[[匝瑳市内循環バス]]、[[横芝光町循環バス]]、[[山武市基幹バス]]、[[東金市内循環バス]]、[[大網白里市コミュニティバス]]、[[茂原市民バス]]、[[長南町巡回バス]]、[[長柄町町民バス]]、[[睦沢町巡回バス]]などの[[コミュニティバス]]がそれぞれの自治体によって運営されているほか、[[空港シャトルバス (成田空港)|空港シャトルバス]]が[[成田国際空港]]方面とを結んでいる<ref>[http://www.npf-airport.jp/news/airportnews/news_0104/topics.html Narita Airport News/ ナリタエアポートニュース - No,95/平成13年4月号]</ref>。

{{日本の平野}} {{デフォルトソート:くしゆうくりへいや}} [[Category:日本の平野]] [[Category:千葉県の地形]] [[Category:房総半島]]

{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a [[plain]] located in the northeastern part of the [[Bōsō Peninsula]], stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=draft}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōgane|ja|東金市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mobara|ja|茂原市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirako, Chiba|ja|白子町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chōsei, Chiba|ja|長生村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chōnan, Chiba|ja|長南町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|ja|一宮町|12=draft}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of [[Chiba Prefecture]].<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a [[coastal plain]], also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=draft}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|12=draft}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=draft}} in {{ill|Isumi|ja|いすみ市|12=draft}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=draft}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=draft}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Jōmon transgression|ja|縄文海進|12=draft}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=draft}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|12=draft}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=draft}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|12=draft}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|12=draft}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|12=draft}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|12=draft}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|12=draft}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|12=draft}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|iron sand|ja|砂鉄|12=draft}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|12=draft}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|12=draft}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|12=draft}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|12=draft}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|12=draft}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|12=draft}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|12=draft}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|12=draft}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|ja|一宮川|12=draft}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|12=draft}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=draft}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|12=draft}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|12=draft}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|12=draft}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tōteiko|ja|洞庭湖|12=draft}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|12=draft}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|12=draft}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|12=draft}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|12=draft}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|12=draft}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|12=draft}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|12=draft}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町|12=draft}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|12=draft}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|12=draft}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|12=draft}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=draft}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=draft}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Naya warehouses|ja|納屋|12=draft}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba Town|ja|横芝町|12=draft}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|qq=draft}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|qq=draft}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|qq=draft}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|qq=draft}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|ja|一宮川|qq=draft}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|qq=draft}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|qq=draft}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōteiko|ja|洞庭湖|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|qq=draft}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|qq=draft}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|qq=draft}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|qq=draft}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|qq=draft}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町|qq=draft}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|qq=draft}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Naya warehouses|ja|納屋|qq=draft}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba Town|ja|横芝町|qq=draft}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

[[ceb:Kuj%C5%ABkuri-heiya]] [[zh:九十里平野]] [[ja:%E4%B9%9D%E5%8D%81%E4%B9%9D%E9%87%8C%E5%B9%B3%E9%87%8E]] [[sn:Kujukuri Plain]]
<!--Kujukuri Plain--><!--Kujukuri Plain-->

{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a [[plain]] located in the northeastern part of the [[Bōsō Peninsula]], stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōgane|ja|東金市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mobara|ja|茂原市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shirako, Chiba|ja|白子町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chōsei, Chiba|ja|長生村|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chōnan, Chiba|ja|長南町|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|ja|一宮町|qq=draft}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of [[Chiba Prefecture]].<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a [[coastal plain]], also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Isumi|ja|いすみ市|qq=draft}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|qq=draft}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|qq=draft}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Jōmon transgression|ja|縄文海進|qq=draft}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|qq=draft}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|qq=draft}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|qq=draft}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|qq=draft}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|qq=draft}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|qq=draft}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|qq=draft}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|qq=draft}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|iron sand|ja|砂鉄|qq=draft}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|qq=draft}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>

Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|qq=draft}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|qq=draft}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町|qq=draft}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|qq=draft}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Naya warehouses|ja|納屋|qq=draft}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba Town|ja|横芝町|qq=draft}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba Town|ja|横芝町|qq=draft}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}}
== Further Reading == * Editorial Committee of the Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary, "Kadokawa Japan Place Names Dictionary, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-04-001120-1. * Research Institute for Japanese Topography, "Japanese Topography Volume 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," Ninomiya Shoten, 1967. * Matsunaga Miyoshi, "Glossary of Folk Geographical Names," Chikuma Shobo, 2021, ISBN 978-4480099303.
== External Links == * Ryōtarō Nakanishi, "[https://doi.org/10.14866/ajg.2017s.0_100100 Regional Study of Coastal Villages in the Kuroshio Current Zone], Japanese Geography Association, Spring 2017. {{doi|10.14866/ajg.2017s.0_100100}}. * Eiichi Nakada, "[https://doi.org/10.14992/00000989 Development of Agricultural Industries in Kujukuri Plain]," Journal of Rikkyo University 1964. {{doi|10.14992/00000989}}.

[[ceb:Kuj%C5%ABkuri-heiya]] [[zh:九十里平野]] [[ja:%E4%B9%9D%E5%8D%81%E4%B9%9D%E9%87%8C%E5%B9%B3%E9%87%8E]] [[sn:Kujukuri Plain]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a [[plain]] located in the northeastern part of the [[Bōsō Peninsula]], stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoshibahikari|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmu|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōgane|ja|東金市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgane|qq=}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|qq=}}, {{ill|Mobara|ja|茂原市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara|qq=}}, {{ill|Shirako, Chiba|ja|白子町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirako, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chōsei, Chiba|ja|長生村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsei, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chōnan, Chiba|ja|長南町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōnan, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|ja|一宮町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya, Chiba|qq=}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of [[Chiba Prefecture]].<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a [[coastal plain]], also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|qq=}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|qq=}} in {{ill|Isumi|ja|いすみ市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isumi|qq=}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|qq=}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|qq=}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Jōmon transgression|ja|縄文海進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon transgression|qq=}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|qq=}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|qq=}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|qq=}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|qq=}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|qq=}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|qq=}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|qq=}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|qq=}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|iron sand|ja|砂鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron sand|qq=}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|qq=}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|qq=}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|qq=}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River|qq=}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|qq=}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|qq=}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|qq=}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|qq=}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|ja|一宮川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya River|qq=}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|qq=}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|qq=}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|qq=}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|qq=}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|qq=}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōteiko|ja|洞庭湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōteiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|qq=}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|qq=}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|qq=}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|qq=}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|qq=}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tako Town|qq=}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|qq=}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoshibahikari|qq=}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmu|qq=}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|qq=}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Naya warehouses|ja|納屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya warehouses|qq=}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba Town|ja|横芝町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoshiba Town|qq=}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />
== References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="note"}}
== Further Reading == * Editorial Committee of the Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary, "Kadokawa Japan Place Names Dictionary, Volume 12: Chiba Prefecture," Kadokawa Shoten, 1984, ISBN 4-04-001120-1. * Research Institute for Japanese Topography, "Japanese Topography Volume 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," Ninomiya Shoten, 1967. * Matsunaga Miyoshi, "Glossary of Folk Geographical Names," Chikuma Shobo, 2021, ISBN 978-4480099303.
== External Links == * Ryōtarō Nakanishi, "[https://doi.org/10.14866/ajg.2017s.0_100100 Regional Study of Coastal Villages in the Kuroshio Current Zone], Japanese Geography Association, Spring 2017. {{doi|10.14866/ajg.2017s.0_100100}}. * Eiichi Nakada, "[https://doi.org/10.14992/00000989 Development of Agricultural Industries in Kujukuri Plain]," Journal of Rikkyo University 1964. {{doi|10.14992/00000989}}.

[[ceb:Kuj%C5%ABkuri-heiya]] [[zh:九十里平野]] [[ja:%E4%B9%9D%E5%8D%81%E4%B9%9D%E9%87%8C%E5%B9%B3%E9%87%8E]] [[sn:Kujukuri Plain]]

revision.text:
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a [[plain]] located in the northeastern part of the [[Bōsō Peninsula]], stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoshibahikari|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|qq=}}, {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmu|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōgane|ja|東金市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōgane|qq=}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|qq=}}, {{ill|Mobara|ja|茂原市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara|qq=}}, {{ill|Shirako, Chiba|ja|白子町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirako, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chōsei, Chiba|ja|長生村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōsei, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chōnan, Chiba|ja|長南町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōnan, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|ja|一宮町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya, Chiba|qq=}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of [[Chiba Prefecture]].<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a [[coastal plain]], also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|qq=}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|ja|旭市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi, Chiba|qq=}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|qq=}} in {{ill|Isumi|ja|いすみ市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isumi|qq=}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|qq=}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|qq=}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Jōmon transgression|ja|縄文海進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōmon transgression|qq=}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|qq=}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|ja|利根川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River|qq=}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|qq=}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|qq=}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|qq=}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|qq=}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|qq=}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|qq=}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|qq=}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|iron sand|ja|砂鉄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron sand|qq=}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|qq=}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|qq=}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|qq=}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|ja|栗山川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River|qq=}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|qq=}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|qq=}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|qq=}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|qq=}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|ja|一宮川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya River|qq=}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|qq=}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|qq=}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|qq=}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|qq=}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|qq=}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|qq=}}, {{ill|Tōteiko|ja|洞庭湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōteiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|qq=}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|qq=}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|qq=}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|qq=}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|qq=}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako Town|ja|多古町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tako Town|qq=}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|qq=}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|ja|横芝光町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoshibahikari|qq=}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}} in {{ill|Sanmu|ja|山武市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanmu|qq=}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|qq=}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Naya warehouses|ja|納屋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya warehouses|qq=}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba Town|ja|横芝町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoshiba Town|qq=}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

[[ceb:Kuj%C5%ABkuri-heiya]] [[zh:九十里平野]] [[ja:%E4%B9%9D%E5%8D%81%E4%B9%9D%E9%87%8C%E5%B9%B3%E9%87%8E]] [[sn:Kujukuri Plain]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a [[plain]] located in the northeastern part of the [[Bōsō Peninsula]], stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|qq=}}, [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]] in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|qq=}}, [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Tōgane|Tōgane]], {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|qq=}}, [[:en:Mobara|Mobara]], [[:en:Shirako|Shirako, Chiba]], [[:en:Chōsei|Chōsei, Chiba]], [[:en:Chōnan|Chōnan, Chiba]], {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ichinomiya, Chiba|Ichinomiya, Chiba]]. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of [[Chiba Prefecture]].<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a [[coastal plain]], also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|qq=}} in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|qq=}} in [[:en:Isumi|Isumi]]. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|qq=}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|qq=}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the [[:en:Holocene glacial retreat|Jōmon transgression]]. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|qq=}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]] due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|qq=}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|qq=}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|qq=}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|qq=}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|qq=}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|qq=}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|qq=}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, [[:en:Ironsand|iron sand]] is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|qq=}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|qq=}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|qq=}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]] (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|qq=}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|qq=}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|qq=}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|qq=}} (116 km²), [[:en:Ichinomiya River|Ichinomiya River]] (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|qq=}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|qq=}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|qq=}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|qq=}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|qq=}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|qq=}}, [[:en:Dongting Lake|Tōteiko]], {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|qq=}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|qq=}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|qq=}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|qq=}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|qq=}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako Town]] in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|qq=}} in the northwest, and [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]] in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}} in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]] near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|qq=}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built [[:en:Barn|Naya warehouses]] to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as [[:en:Yokoshiba, Chiba|Yokoshiba Town]], the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|qq=}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|qq=}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|qq=}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|qq=}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|qq=}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|qq=}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, [[:en:Ironsand|iron sand]] is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|qq=}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|qq=}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|qq=}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: [[:en:Kuriyama River|Kuriyama River]] (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|qq=}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|qq=}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|qq=}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|qq=}} (116 km²), [[:en:Ichinomiya River|Ichinomiya River]] (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|qq=}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|qq=}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|qq=}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|qq=}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|qq=}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|qq=}}, [[:en:Dongting Lake|Tōteiko]], {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|qq=}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|qq=}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|qq=}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|qq=}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|qq=}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|qq=}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|qq=}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by [[:en:Tako, Chiba|Tako Town]] in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|qq=}} in the northwest, and [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]] in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|qq=}} in [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]] near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|qq=}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|qq=}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built [[:en:Barn|Naya warehouses]] to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as [[:en:Yokoshiba, Chiba|Yokoshiba Town]], the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

[[ceb:Kuj%C5%ABkuri-heiya]] [[zh:九十里平野]] [[ja:%E4%B9%9D%E5%8D%81%E4%B9%9D%E9%87%8C%E5%B9%B3%E9%87%8E]] [[sn:Kujukuri Plain]]
<!--Kujukuri Plain--><!--Kujukuri Plain--> [[Category:房総半島]]

{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a [[plain]] located in the northeastern part of the [[Bōsō Peninsula]], stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|qq=}}, [[:en:Yokoshibahikari|Yokoshibahikari]] in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|qq=}}, [[:en:Sanmu|Sanmu]], [[:en:Tōgane|Tōgane]], {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|qq=}}, [[:en:Mobara|Mobara]], [[:en:Shirako|Shirako, Chiba]], [[:en:Chōsei|Chōsei, Chiba]], [[:en:Chōnan|Chōnan, Chiba]], {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|qq=}}, and [[:en:Ichinomiya, Chiba|Ichinomiya, Chiba]]. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of [[Chiba Prefecture]].<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a [[coastal plain]], also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|qq=}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|qq=}} in [[:en:Asahi, Chiba|Asahi, Chiba]], and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|qq=}} in [[:en:Isumi|Isumi]]. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|qq=}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|qq=}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the [[:en:Holocene glacial retreat|Jōmon transgression]]. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|qq=}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the [[:en:Tone River|Tone River]] due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|qq=}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|qq=}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|qq=}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|qq=}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces [[iodine]] from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|qq=}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|qq=}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|qq=}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, [[:en:Ironsand|iron sand]] is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|qq=}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|qq=}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|qq=}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
