{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|WD=Q160091|lt=Plain}} located in the northeastern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula}}, stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Tōgane|en|Tōgane|WD=Q637913|lt=Tōgane}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|WD=Q502486|lt=Mobara}}, {{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chōsei|en|Chōsei|WD=Q1202647|lt=Chōsei, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chōnan|en|Chōnan|WD=Q1203194|lt=Chōnan, Chiba}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|en|Ichinomiya, Chiba|WD=Q1203602|lt=Ichinomiya, Chiba}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a {{ill|coastal plain|en|coastal plain|WD=Q31566|lt=coastal plain}}, also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Isumi|en|Isumi|WD=Q840834|lt=Isumi}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[:en:sand dune|sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Holocene glacial retreat|en|Holocene glacial retreat|WD=Q834405|lt=Jōmon transgression}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九里浜|1=Kujūkuri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996212}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|1=Sea Opening|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559379}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[:en:gas field|gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces {{ill|iodine|en|iodine|WD=Q1103|lt=iodine}} from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|Ironsand|en|Ironsand|WD=Q1394005|lt=iron sand}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Kidogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|1=Sakuta River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385396}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|en|Ichinomiya River|WD=Q5986256|lt=Ichinomiya River}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495489}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖沼|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q337567}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|1=Kusayanuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370678}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|1=Sakataike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425526}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Dongting Lake|en|Dongting Lake|WD=Q216083|lt=Tōteiko}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|1=Konaka Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458767}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|1=Ōtone irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433046}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q337567}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495489}} in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Barn|en|Barn|WD=Q1303167|lt=Naya warehouses}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba, Chiba|en|Yokoshiba, Chiba|WD=Q5120369|lt=Yokoshiba Town}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]]
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|WD=Q160091|lt=Plain|1=Plain}} located in the northeastern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}, stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Tōgane|en|Tōgane|WD=Q637913|lt=Tōgane|1=Tōgane}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|WD=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako, Chiba|1=Shirako}}, {{ill|Chōsei|en|Chōsei|WD=Q1202647|lt=Chōsei, Chiba|1=Chōsei}}, {{ill|Chōnan|en|Chōnan|WD=Q1203194|lt=Chōnan, Chiba|1=Chōnan}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|en|Ichinomiya, Chiba|WD=Q1203602|lt=Ichinomiya, Chiba|1=Ichinomiya, Chiba}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a {{ill|coastal plain|en|coastal plain|WD=Q31566|lt=coastal plain|1=Coastal plain}}, also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Isumi|en|Isumi|WD=Q840834|lt=Isumi|1=Isumi}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[:en:sand dune|sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Holocene glacial retreat|en|Holocene glacial retreat|WD=Q834405|lt=Jōmon transgression|1=Holocene glacial retreat}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九里浜|1=Kujūkuri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996212}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|1=umibiraki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559379}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[:en:gas field|gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces {{ill|iodine|en|iodine|WD=Q1103|lt=iodine|1=Iodine}} from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|Ironsand|en|Ironsand|WD=Q1394005|lt=iron sand|1=Ironsand}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River|1=Kuriyama River}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Q11518414|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|1=Sakuta River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385396}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|en|Ichinomiya River|WD=Q5986256|lt=Ichinomiya River|1=Ichinomiya River}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495489}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖沼|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q337567}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|1=Q11370678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370678}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|1=Q11425526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425526}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Dongting Lake|en|Dongting Lake|WD=Q216083|lt=Tōteiko|1=Dongting Lake}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|1=Konaka Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458767}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|1=Ōtone irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433046}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q337567}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town|1=Tako, Chiba}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495489}} in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Barn|en|Barn|WD=Q1303167|lt=Naya warehouses|1=Barn}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba, Chiba|en|Yokoshiba, Chiba|WD=Q5120369|lt=Yokoshiba Town|1=Yokoshiba, Chiba}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]] [[ja:九十九里平野]] {{wikidata link|Q5120855}} [[Category:Bōsō Peninsula]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Geography of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Landforms of Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Plains of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]]
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|WD=Q160091|lt=Plain|1=Plain}} located in the northeastern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}, stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Tōgane|en|Tōgane|WD=Q637913|lt=Tōgane|1=Tōgane}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|WD=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako, Chiba|1=Shirako}}, {{ill|Chōsei|en|Chōsei|WD=Q1202647|lt=Chōsei, Chiba|1=Chōsei}}, {{ill|Chōnan|en|Chōnan|WD=Q1203194|lt=Chōnan, Chiba|1=Chōnan}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|en|Ichinomiya, Chiba|WD=Q1203602|lt=Ichinomiya, Chiba|1=Ichinomiya, Chiba}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a {{ill|coastal plain|en|coastal plain|WD=Q31566|lt=coastal plain|1=Coastal plain}}, also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Isumi|en|Isumi|WD=Q840834|lt=Isumi|1=Isumi}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[:en:sand dune|sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Holocene glacial retreat|en|Holocene glacial retreat|WD=Q834405|lt=Jōmon transgression|1=Holocene glacial retreat}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九里浜|1=Kujūkuri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996212}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|1=umibiraki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559379}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[:en:gas field|gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces {{ill|iodine|en|iodine|WD=Q1103|lt=iodine|1=Iodine}} from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|Ironsand|en|Ironsand|WD=Q1394005|lt=iron sand|1=Ironsand}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>

Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Barn|en|Barn|WD=Q1303167|lt=Naya warehouses|1=Barn}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba, Chiba|en|Yokoshiba, Chiba|WD=Q5120369|lt=Yokoshiba Town|1=Yokoshiba, Chiba}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|WD=Q160091|lt=Plain|1=Plain}} located in the northeastern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|WD=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}, stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Tōgane|en|Tōgane|WD=Q637913|lt=Tōgane|1=Tōgane}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|WD=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako, Chiba|1=Shirako}}, {{ill|Chōsei|en|Chōsei|WD=Q1202647|lt=Chōsei, Chiba|1=Chōsei}}, {{ill|Chōnan|en|Chōnan|WD=Q1203194|lt=Chōnan, Chiba|1=Chōnan}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|en|Ichinomiya, Chiba|WD=Q1203602|lt=Ichinomiya, Chiba|1=Ichinomiya, Chiba}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a {{ill|coastal plain|en|coastal plain|WD=Q31566|lt=coastal plain|1=Coastal plain}}, also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|WD=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Isumi|en|Isumi|WD=Q840834|lt=Isumi|1=Isumi}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[:en:sand dune|sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Holocene glacial retreat|en|Holocene glacial retreat|WD=Q834405|lt=Jōmon transgression|1=Holocene glacial retreat}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九里浜|1=Kujūkuri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q996212}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|1=umibiraki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559379}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[:en:gas field|gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces {{ill|iodine|en|iodine|WD=Q1103|lt=iodine|1=Iodine}} from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|Ironsand|en|Ironsand|WD=Q1394005|lt=iron sand|1=Ironsand}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|WD=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River|1=Kuriyama River}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Q11518414|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518414}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|1=Sakuta River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385396}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|en|Ichinomiya River|WD=Q5986256|lt=Ichinomiya River|1=Ichinomiya River}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495489}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖沼|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q337567}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|1=Q11370678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370678}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|1=Q11425526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425526}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Dongting Lake|en|Dongting Lake|WD=Q216083|lt=Tōteiko|1=Dongting Lake}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|1=Konaka Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458767}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|1=Ōtone irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433046}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q337567}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|WD=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town|1=Tako, Chiba}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|WD=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495489}} in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|WD=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Barn|en|Barn|WD=Q1303167|lt=Naya warehouses|1=Barn}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba, Chiba|en|Yokoshiba, Chiba|WD=Q5120369|lt=Yokoshiba Town|1=Yokoshiba, Chiba}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Kujūkuri-heiya]][[ja:九十九里平野]]
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|qid=Q160091|lt=Plain|1=Plain}} located in the northeastern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}, stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|qid=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Tōgane|en|Tōgane|qid=Q637913|lt=Tōgane|1=Tōgane}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|qid=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|qid=Q1203293|lt=Shirako, Chiba|1=Shirako}}, {{ill|Chōsei|en|Chōsei|qid=Q1202647|lt=Chōsei, Chiba|1=Chōsei}}, {{ill|Chōnan|en|Chōnan|qid=Q1203194|lt=Chōnan, Chiba|1=Chōnan}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|en|Ichinomiya, Chiba|qid=Q1203602|lt=Ichinomiya, Chiba|1=Ichinomiya, Chiba}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a {{ill|coastal plain|en|coastal plain|qid=Q31566|lt=coastal plain|1=Coastal plain}}, also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|qid=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Isumi|en|Isumi|qid=Q840834|lt=Isumi|1=Isumi}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[:en:sand dune|sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Holocene glacial retreat|en|Holocene glacial retreat|qid=Q834405|lt=Jōmon transgression|1=Holocene glacial retreat}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九里浜|1=Kujūkuri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q996212}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|qid=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。

Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495489}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖沼|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q337567}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|1=Q11370678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370678}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|1=Q11425526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425526}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Dongting Lake|en|Dongting Lake|qid=Q216083|lt=Tōteiko|1=Dongting Lake}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|1=Konaka Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11458767}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|1=Ōtone irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433046}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q337567}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|qid=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town|1=Tako, Chiba}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495489}} in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Barn|en|Barn|qid=Q1303167|lt=Naya warehouses|1=Barn}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba, Chiba|en|Yokoshiba, Chiba|qid=Q5120369|lt=Yokoshiba Town|1=Yokoshiba, Chiba}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 10 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template (has duplicate-language pairs) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/25098527498|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{File clip | Geofeatures map of Kanto Japan ja.svg | width = 250 | 55 | 4 | 10 | 55 | w = 700 | h = 700 | Kujukuri Plain and Bōsō Peninsula Geofeatures}} '''Kujukuri Plain''' (九十里平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|qid=Q160091|lt=Plain|1=Plain}} located in the northeastern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}, stretching behind the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It spans six cities, six towns, and one village, including {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|qid=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sōsa, Chiba|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in {{ill|Sanbu District|ja|山武部地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanbu District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}}, {{ill|Tōgane|en|Tōgane|qid=Q637913|lt=Tōgane|1=Tōgane}}, {{ill|Kujukuri, Chiba|ja|九十里町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōami-shirasato|ja|大羽白里市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōami-shirasato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mobara|en|Mobara|qid=Q502486|lt=Mobara|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|qid=Q1203293|lt=Shirako, Chiba|1=Shirako}}, {{ill|Chōsei|en|Chōsei|qid=Q1202647|lt=Chōsei, Chiba|1=Chōsei}}, {{ill|Chōnan|en|Chōnan|qid=Q1203194|lt=Chōnan, Chiba|1=Chōnan}}, {{ill|Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja|睦澤町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuzawa, Chiba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichinomiya, Chiba|en|Ichinomiya, Chiba|qid=Q1203602|lt=Ichinomiya, Chiba|1=Ichinomiya, Chiba}}. Its total area covers approximately 900 square kilometers, accounting for 20% of {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}.<ref name="kd" />
== Geography == [[File:Touki Ryushichi 1926a 21.png|thumb|right|350px|A 1926 map of the Kantō Plain overlaid with the Jōmon marine transgression area (shaded region)]] The Kujukuri Plain extends about 60 kilometers from north to south and 10 kilometers from east to west. It is a {{ill|coastal plain|en|coastal plain|qid=Q31566|lt=coastal plain|1=Coastal plain}}, also known as the Kujukuri Alluvial Lowland<ref group="note">Here, "alluvial" refers to the Holocene epoch in the geological time scale.</ref>. This elongated plain curves along the {{ill|Kujukuri Beach|ja|九十里浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, forming a long, arc-shaped rectangle. Its northeastern end is near {{ill|Gyōbu Cape|ja|刑部嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbu Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Asahi, Chiba|en|Asahi, Chiba|qid=Q637868|lt=Asahi, Chiba|1=Asahi, Chiba}}, and its southern end is near {{ill|Taitō Cape|ja|太東嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taitō Cape|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Isumi|en|Isumi|qid=Q840834|lt=Isumi|1=Isumi}}. To the north, it borders the {{ill|Shimousa Plateau|ja|下縄台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimousa Plateau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while to the west, it meets the {{ill|Bōsō Hills|ja|房羽山地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Hills|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its elevation does not exceed 10 meters, with old [[:en:sand dune|sand dune]] ridges running parallel to the coastline.
This plain was formed as the coastline retreated after the {{ill|Holocene glacial retreat|en|Holocene glacial retreat|qid=Q834405|lt=Jōmon transgression|1=Holocene glacial retreat}}. Before the [[Middle Ages of Japan|Middle Ages]], it was called {{ill|Tama no Ura|ja|玉の浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama no Ura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 九十九里浜|1=Kujūkuri Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q996212}} and was part of the sea. Even today, the salt concentration in the scattered ponds and marshes shows a gradient from inland to the coast.
Coastal retreat has now ceased, but a reduction in sandy beaches has been observed. This is attributed to decreased sediment flow from the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|qid=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}} due to river engineering, as well as human efforts to prevent erosion at {{ill|Byōbugaura|ja|布屋浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōbugaura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Taitō Cape. <ref name="kd">『角川日本地名大辞典 12 千葉県』 329頁</ref>。
== Climate == This region is representative of areas on the Pacific coast of Japan. Due to its coastal location in the south, it has a warm climate with minimal daily and annual temperature variation. In Japan’s independent climate classification, it is categorized as {{ill|Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja|太平洋岸式気候|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific Oceanic Climate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Unaffected by heat islands, it experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it a pleasant place to live. As such, it attracts tourists seeking summer resorts ({{ill|beach opening|ja|海開き|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/beach opening|1=umibiraki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11559379}}) and winter retreats.
== Resources == The Kujukuri Plain lies within the {{ill|Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja|南関東ガス町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southern Kantō Gas Field|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan’s largest water-soluble natural [[:en:gas field|gas field]], with recoverable reserves reaching 375 billion cubic meters. In addition to natural gas, this field produces {{ill|iodine|en|iodine|qid=Q1103|lt=iodine|1=Iodine}} from the brine within the rock layers, containing iodine concentrations approximately 2,000 times that of seawater.<ref group="note">At one point, iodine from the Southern Kantō Gas Field accounted for nearly 40% of the world’s production, making Japan the world’s largest iodine producer for many years. It is now second to Chile, which produces iodine from {{ill|caliche|ja|チリ砂石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/caliche|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref>
Natural gas extraction in the {{ill|Tokyo Bay area|ja|東京湾岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Bay area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceased in the 1970s (Shōwa 45) due to severe land subsidence. Although land subsidence remains an issue in the Kujukuri Plain, extraction continues under agreements between municipalities and extraction companies. Many municipalities also operate [[city gas]] supply services.
In areas where gas layers are shallow, natural gas sometimes seeps from ground cracks and ignites. The explosion at the {{ill|Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja|九十里いわし博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujukuri Sardine Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2004 (Heisei 16) was attributed to ignited naturally-emitted gas.
Although not as prominent as natural gas, {{ill|Ironsand|en|Ironsand|qid=Q1394005|lt=iron sand|1=Ironsand}} is another mineral resource found here. The Kujukuri Plain became land due to seabed uplift and sandbar formation originating from the {{ill|Inubōsaki|ja|犬吠嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inubōsaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} area. Sand with low specific gravity was carried southward by tidal currents, while heavier iron sand remained in the north. High concentrations of iron sand were mined near {{ill|Iioka|ja|飼おか町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, supplying 198,000 tons in 1964 to the Chiba Plant of {{ill|Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja|東部化学工業|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhō Chemical Industry|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>"Nihon Chishi Vol. 8: Chiba and Kanagawa Prefectures," p. 53</ref>
== Land Use == === Water Systems and Fishing Ports === * Rivers: {{ill|Kuriyama River|en|Kuriyama River|qid=Q6446101|lt=Kuriyama River|1=Kuriyama River}} (Basin area: 292 km²), {{ill|Kidogawa River|ja|木戸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kidogawa River|1=Q11518414|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11518414}} (72 km²), {{ill|Sakuta River|ja|作田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuta River|1=Sakuta River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11385396}} (104 km²), {{ill|Makame River|ja|真丸川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makame River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (82 km²), {{ill|Nanjirogawa River|ja|南白鱗川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjirogawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (116 km²), {{ill|Ichinomiya River|en|Ichinomiya River|qid=Q5986256|lt=Ichinomiya River|1=Ichinomiya River}} (222 km²) * Wetlands: Kuriyama River Wetlands ({{ill|Takohikari Wetlands|ja|多古光水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takohikari Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495489}}, {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * {{ill|Lagoon|ja|海跡湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lagoon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 湖沼|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q337567}}: {{ill|Tsubakiko|ja|椊海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared), {{ill|Kusayanuma|ja|乾草沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusayanuma|1=Q11370678|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370678}}, {{ill|Sakataike|ja|坂田池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakataike|1=Q11425526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425526}}, {{ill|Toriikunuma|ja|鳥食沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriikunuma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (disappeared) * Reservoirs: {{ill|Jakagaike|ja|雀蛇ヶ池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jakagaike|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Dongting Lake|en|Dongting Lake|qid=Q216083|lt=Tōteiko|1=Dongting Lake}}, {{ill|Konakaike|ja|小中池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakaike|1=Konaka Pond|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11458767}} * Canals: {{ill|Great Tone Canal|ja|大利根用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tone Canal|1=Ōtone irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433046}}, {{ill|Ryōsō Canal|ja|両縄用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōsō Canal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Bōsō Conduit|ja|房羽導水路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Conduit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Fishing Ports: {{ill|Iioka Fishing Port|ja|飼おか渡船港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iioka Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja|栗山川港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuriyama River Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katakai Fishing Port|ja|片谷港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katakai Fishing Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Central Kujukuri Plain contains midstream wetlands of the Kuriyama River, remnants of Jōmon-period {{ill|lagoon|ja|湖沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lagoon|1=still waters|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q337567}}s, known as the Kuriyama River Wetlands. This area hosts endangered wetland plant species but has rapidly decreased due to {{ill|Meiji-period development|ja|明治期高速開発|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji-period development|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="note">While many lagoons once existed in the Kujukuri Plain, few remain in the Kuriyama River midstream, bordered by {{ill|Tako, Chiba|en|Tako, Chiba|qid=Q1202807|lt=Tako Town|1=Tako, Chiba}} in the south, {{ill|Sōsa|ja|匿哲市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the northwest, and {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}} in the north.</ref> The {{ill|Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja|成東東金食虫植物群落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narutō-Tōgane Carnivorous Plant Community|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 成東・東金食虫植物群落|1=Naruto-Togane Area Carnivorous Plant Field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495489}} in {{ill|Sanmu|en|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|lt=Sanmu|1=Sanmu}} near the Tōgane-Sakuta river boundary is a National Natural Monument. Dr. Tomitarō Makino, a botanist, praised the {{ill|Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja|茂原八積水原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara-Hachitsumi Wetlands|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a "treasure trove of plants."
=== Naya Settlements === The Kujukuri Plain has unique {{ill|Naya Settlement Patterns|ja|納屋集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naya Settlement Patterns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Villages and farmland alternate in a linear pattern parallel to the coastline. These settlements started when fishermen near coasts transitioned to farmers and built {{ill|Barn|en|Barn|qid=Q1303167|lt=Naya warehouses|1=Barn}} to store gear. Later, as coastlines retreated, they settled permanently. During consolidation into broader towns, such as {{ill|Yokoshiba, Chiba|en|Yokoshiba, Chiba|qid=Q5120369|lt=Yokoshiba Town|1=Yokoshiba, Chiba}}, the shape of their municipal areas often remained elongated.<ref name="kd" />
